text:
Assistant: No, there isn't. `providers.json`/`llms.json` (which drive model selection per role, e.g. `edge-distillation`) live under `~/.tenex-edge` and are read-only from tenex-edge's side — you'd edit them by hand. There's no `tenex-edge` subcommand or TUI to manage them; the only TUI in the codebase (`tmux_cli/tui_*.rs`) is the session-spawner picker, unrelated to model config. [...] - **`store.rs`** — reads/writes `providers.json`/`llms.json` as raw JSON (preserves unknown keys — these files are shared with the wider TENEX format), with unit tests [...] 3. **Confirm-before-write is a semantic summary, not a JSON diff**: `edge-distillation → qwen2.5:7b (ollama)  was: mistral:7b`, default Yes; answering No returns to the previous prompt with state intact. Atomic writes, unknown JSON keys round-tripped, secrets always shown truncated (`sk-or-…4f2a`) everywhere including receipts and errors. Removing a provider warns which roles break but leaves them visibly dangling rather than auto-deleting. [...] On your question — no, providers/models aren't the only configurable things, they're just the only two I built. `config.json` (separate from `providers.json`/`llms.json`) has several more knobs currently only editable by hand:

- `relays` / `indexerRelay` — which Nostr relays to use
- `backendName` — the host label published on this agent's profile
- `userNsec` / `tenexPrivateKey` — the operator's and backend's signing keys
- `tmuxStatusCommand` — custom tmux status-bar format
- `perSessionRooms` — whether human sessions mint their own NIP-29 subgroup
- `whitelistedPubkeys` — the admin/trust list
